title: calamagrostis matsumurae var. exaristata
intro: Calamagrostis matsumurae var. exaristata, also known as Japanese feather-reed grass, is a type of ornamental grass native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating pain, as well as promoting digestion and relieving respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Calamagrostis matsumurae var. exaristata typically involve decocting the roots and rhizomes to create teas or infusions for medicinal use.
